Question:
불행히도,
물은 어디에서도 찾을 수 없었다.
Answer:
Unfortunately,
water was nowhere to be found.
Key Word:
nowhere (adv.)
Nowhere means no place or not existing.

Question:
나는 로션을 톡톡 두드려 발랐다
내 얼굴에.
Answer:
I patted some lotion
onto my face.
Key Word:
pat (v.)
To pat something is to hit it softly with your hand.

Question:
나는 안도감을 느꼈다
그 좋은 소식을 들었을 때.
Answer:
I felt a sense of relief
when I heard the good news.
Key Word:
relief (n.)
Relief is a good feeling after something bad or challenging ends.

Question:
그 아이들은 자기 집을 똑같이 만들려고 했다
장난감 블록을 사용해서.
Answer:
The children tried to reproduce their house
using toy blocks.
Key Word:
reproduce (v.)
To reproduce is to make something exactly how someone else did it.

Question:
"험프티 덤프티"는 오래된 운율이다
아이들이 학교에서 배우는.
Answer:
"Humpty Dumpty" is an old rhyme
that children learn in school.
Key Word:
rhyme (n.)
A rhyme is writing or speech that has words with the same ending sounds.

Question:
그 아기는 우유를 빨았다
젖병에서.
Answer:
The baby sucked milk
from her bottle.
Key Word:
suck (v.)
To suck is to put something in your mouth and try to pull something out of it.

Question:
그는 그 순간 떠나야 했다;
그것은 긴급했다.
Answer:
He had to leave at that moment;
it was urgent.
Key Word:
urgent (adj.)
An urgent thing is important and needs to be done now.

Question:
모든 승객들이
기차역에서 사라졌다.
Answer:
All the passengers
vanished from the train station.
Key Word:
vanish (v.)
To vanish is to go away suddenly.

Question:
그는 그의 수레를 사용했다
자기 선물 일부를 옮기기 위해.
Answer:
He used his wagon
to carry some of his gifts.
Key Word:
wagon (n.)
A wagon is a cart used to carry heavy things.

Question:
내 할아버지는
얼굴에 주름이 좀 있다.
Answer:
My grandfather
has some wrinkles on his face.
Key Word:
wrinkle (n.)
A wrinkle is a line on a person's face that appears as they get old.
